title: elymus virginicus var. intermedius
intro: Elymus virginicus var. intermedius, also known as the intermediate wildrye, is a type of perennial grass native to the eastern United States. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of Elymus virginicus var. intermedius include reducing inflammation and relieving digestive issues, as well as supporting immune function and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
